text: Artificial intelligence is changing our world rapidly, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias: if we train AI on data that includes human prejudices, the AI may make unfair decisions. This challenges fairness, especially in areas like hiring or lending. Privacy is another issue—AI systems often collect vast amounts of personal data, which must be protected. Transparency is crucial too; people should understand how AI reaches its conclusions. To address these problems, responsible AI development requires careful testing, diverse teams, and clear guidelines. Ultimately, we must balance innovation with ethical principles to build technology that benefits everyone fairly.
